result:
The R script has been successfully executed and the cleaned data file has been created. Here's a summary of what was accomplished:

## Output Summary

**File Created:** `data/output/cleaned_data.csv`

**Dataset Dimensions:**
- **Rows:** 14,586 participants
- **Columns:** 9 (1 ID + 8 GHQ variables)

**Variables in Output:**
| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| NSID | Cohort member identifier |
| ghq15 | Pre-derived GHQ-12 score at age 15 |
| ghq17 | Pre-derived GHQ-12 score at age 17 |
| ghq25 | Pre-derived GHQ-12 score at age 25 |
| ghq32 | Pre-derived GHQ-12 score at age 32 |
| ghqtl15 | Manually computed GHQ-12 sum at age 15 |
| ghqtl17 | Manually computed GHQ-12 sum at age 17 |
| ghqtl25 | Manually computed GHQ-12 sum at age 25 |
| ghqtl32 | Manually computed GHQ-12 sum at age 32 |

**Processing Steps Completed:**
1. ✅ Loaded all 6 input files from `data/input/`
2. ✅ Extracted GHQ-12 items and pre-derived scores from each sweep
3. ✅ Merged datasets using `full_join` by NSID
4. ✅ Harmonized pre-derived scores (ghqXX) with standard missing codes
5. ✅ Computed manual sum scores (ghqtlXX) from item-level data
6. ✅ Applied standard missing-value code labels
7. ✅ Converted all GHQ variables to factors
8. ✅ Output to CSV format

**Sample Output:**
```
# A tibble: 6 × 9
  NSID     ghq15 ghq17 ghq25 ghq32 ghqtl15 ghqtl17 ghqtl25 ghqtl32
  <chr>    <fct> <fct> <fct> <fct> <fct>   <fct>   <fct>   <fct>  
1 NS10001S -9    0     0     -3    -8      17      19      -3     
2 NS10002T 3     5     -3    0     22      30      -3      21     
3 NS10003V 0     0     -3    -3    21      18      -3      -3     
4 NS10004W 4     1     6     0     27      22      29      19     
5 NS10005X 2     -3    -3    -3    24      -3      -3      -3     
6 NS10006Y 0     0     -3    7     18      18      -3      31     
```

The data is now analysis-ready with consistent variable naming, harmonized missing-value codes, and all GHQ variables converted to factors for easy use in statistical modeling.
